title: Starbirth shuts down 40,000 light-years from the Milky Way's core — and astronomers don't know why
summary: Astronomers discovered the boundary of star formation in the Milky Way's spiral disk, located closer to the galaxy's core than expected. The reason behind this unexpected shutdown remains unknown.

title: NASA chief Jared Isaacman says he's fighting for Pluto: 'I am very much in the camp of 'make Pluto a planet again'
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman advocates for Pluto's reclassification as a planet, stating he supports the movement to 'make Pluto a planet again'. NASA is reportedly revisiting the discussion on Pluto's planetary status.

title: These astronauts are trying to uphold the US Constitution: 'We need to make sure that people are using facts and evidence'
summary: Astronauts emphasize the importance of using facts and evidence while exploring space. They highlight how space exploration can inspire wonder and open minds.

title: Artemis 3 SLS rocket's core stage arrives in Florida for 2027 launch (photo)
summary: The core stage of NASA's Artemis 3 SLS rocket has arrived at Kennedy Space Center in Florida after a 900-mile barge journey. The rocket is scheduled for a 2027 launch as part of NASA's Artemis program.

title: Clean up Rebel scum in the upcoming Star Wars DLC for PowerWash Simulator 2
summary: A new Star Wars-themed DLC for PowerWash Simulator 2 has been announced, featuring Rebel characters from the franchise. Players will use soap and water to clean up iconic Star Wars scenes.

title: Canadian astronaut Josh Kutryk finally flying to ISS after Boeing Starliner mishap: 'I'm committed to making the most of this unique opportunity'
summary: Canadian astronaut Josh Kutryk has been assigned to the Crew-13 mission to the ISS after the Boeing Starliner mishap delayed his original flight. This comes shortly after Canada's involvement in the Artemis 2 moon mission.

title: Artemis 2 heat shield is a sunken treasure in the ocean | Space photo of the day for April 28, 2026
summary: The heat shield from Artemis 2 is highlighted as a notable artifact after splashdown. The image captures the heat shield floating in the ocean.

title: Aurora expert captures rare pulsating northern lights in remarkable detail: 'One of the most profound sightings of my career'
summary: An expert captured rare pulsating northern lights in Arctic Norway during a powerful geomagnetic storm lasting nearly three hours. The display was described as one of the most profound sightings of the expert's career.

title: Scientists learn how much baby stars in Orion weigh — by watching their dance moves
summary: Scientists measured the masses of young binary stars in Orion's star-forming region by observing their movements, validating theoretical models. Radio astronomers used deep observations of gas clouds to achieve this breakthrough.

title: NASA Connects Little Red Dots with Chandra, Webb
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory discovered a new object that may help explain mysterious 'X-ray dots' found in the early universe. The discovery could clarify the nature of hundreds or thousands of similar objects detected in recent years.

title: There’s No Place Like NASA’s New X-59 Hangar Home
summary: NASA's newly renovated X-59 hangar at Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, now houses the quiet supersonic aircraft after a full renovation of the 1968 facility.

title: Nighttime Imaging Grows Landsat’s Science Value
summary: Landsat satellites have been imaging Earth's land and near-shore surfaces for over 50 years during midmorning orbits. Now, Landsat 8 and Landsat 9 are also utilizing their ascending paths to enhance nighttime imaging capabilities.
company: Earth Resources Observation and Science (EROS) Center

title: Curiosity Captures a 360-Degree View at ‘Nevado Sajama’
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover captured a 360-degree view of boxwork formations on Mars between November 9 and December 7, 2025. This 1.5-billion-pixel panorama is one of the largest images the rover has ever taken.

title: NASA Fires Up Powerful Lithium-Fed Thruster for Trips to Mars
summary: NASA successfully tested a powerful lithium-fed thruster at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory, potentially enabling crewed missions to Mars and robotic exploration across the solar system. The test marked the first high-power operation of such a thruster in the U.S. in years.

title: NASA Laser Terminal Enhances Views During Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's laser terminal improved views during the Artemis II mission, allowing millions to witness the historic lunar journey of four astronauts. The technology enhanced public engagement with the mission's 10-day circumlunar flight.

title: Six Years of Curiosity’s Wheels on the Move
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover captured a timelapse of its six years of driving using its right navigation camera. The images span from January 2, 2020, to March 8, 2026, covering Martian days 2,633 to 4,830.

title: Science in Space
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot are collaborating in the Kibo lab to process genetic samples for the DNA Nano Therapeutics-3 experiment, which investigates DNA-inspired techniques for manufacturing cancer treatments.

title: NASA’s X-59 Gets Freedom 250 Logo
summary: NASA's X-59 aircraft has received a special livery to commemorate the U.S. 250th independence anniversary. The aircraft is part of NASA's Quesst mission to demonstrate quiet supersonic flight.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent Artemis II launch, which carried four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Arianespace's Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites in six launches across Florida, California, and French Guiana. This marks the return of the Falcon Heavy and highlights the growing demand for satellite launches.

title: SpaceX scrubs launch of Falcon Heavy on Monday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX canceled the launch of its Falcon Heavy rocket, which had not flown in over 18 months. The scrub marks a delay for the heavy-lift vehicle's return to flight.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic compounds on Mars, providing fresh clues about ancient life on the planet. This finding marks a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission two weeks prior.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship Block 3 at the Cape, with hopes to launch it this year. The first flight of Block 3 is approaching as the company advances its development.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and progress of developing new spacesuits for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled across four countries using five different launch vehicles during the week of April 20. This marks a busy period for spaceflight activities globally.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This follows Blue Origin's recent achievement of landing a previously flown first-stage booster.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This marks a significant milestone in the development of partially reusable heavy-lift launch vehicles.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website has reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation aims to enhance satellite servicing and debris removal operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, putting it on track for a launch in early 2027 to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum in 2026, including an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9. The forum aims to gather feedback from these enterprises on topics affecting their work in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received from an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the satellite navigation system.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, capturing over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission's first results reveal solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or new methods for directional control. It highlights the significance of these innovations for mission success and efficiency.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team conducted experiments on graphene aerogels using lasers during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025, observing significant propulsion in microgravity conditions. The findings, published in Advanced Science, suggest potential applications for light-based propulsion in steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, according to space executives. Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon emphasized that it aims to offer no unique differentiators compared to existing commercial satellite buses.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump over proposed cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it harmful to science and innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Isaacman Continues to Defend Budget Cuts to Congress
summary: Isaacman defended budget cuts to Congress while emphasizing NASA's priority to accelerate the US return to the Moon. The hearing highlighted the agency's top objective amid financial adjustments.

title: True Anomaly Closes $650M Series D
summary: True Anomaly, a four-year-old space company, raised $650 million in a Series D funding round. The company also announced its Golden Dome award for space-based interceptor technology.

title: Meta Secures Overview Energy Space Solar Power Capacity
summary: Meta has secured an agreement to obtain up to 1 GW of power capacity through Overview Energy's planned solar power-beaming satellite system. This initiative aims to support Meta's energy needs via space-based solar power technology.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon has selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI Tech program, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion in total. This initiative aims to enhance space-based defense capabilities.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to scale operations and reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The initiative is part of Astra's strategy to enhance its launch capabilities.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Donald Trump has appointed two space industry executives to military space roles. The nominees require Senate confirmation before assuming their positions.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, potentially powering future Moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: A cost analysis compares the build costs of an orbital data center with a terrestrial one, including opportunity costs. The study is presented in a Payload Pro article.

title: True Anomaly Raises $650 Million in New Round to Continue Scaling
summary: True Anomaly secured $650 million in a new funding round to expand its workforce and capabilities, aiming to meet growing customer demand.

title: ST Engineering Signs Singapore Public Safety Deal with HTX
summary: ST Engineering and HTX will establish a new space technology program to co-develop capabilities for Singapore's public safety operations. The collaboration aims to strengthen national safety through space-based science and technology.

title: MaiaSpace CEO Says “Start Later, Run Faster” as Launcher Works Toward First Mission
summary: MaiaSpace, a French launcher developer, aims to disrupt the industry by adopting a 'start later, run faster' approach to bring a new rocket to market for European launch services. The company is progressing toward its first mission despite industry trends favoring earlier development starts.

title: Airbus Defence and Space Improves Revenue and Margin in Q1 2026
summary: Airbus Defence and Space reported improved revenue, adjusted EBIT, and order intake in Q1 2026, nearly doubling its order intake compared to the same period last year.

title: T-Mobile Debuts Managed Broadband Service for Businesses, Combining 5G and Starlink
summary: T-Mobile has launched a new managed broadband service for businesses called Super Broadband, combining 5G and Starlink satellite internet. This partnership deepens T-Mobile's collaboration with SpaceX.

title: ULA Launches 29 Satellites in 6th Mission for Amazon Leo
summary: United Launch Alliance (ULA) launched 29 satellites for Amazon's Leo constellation using an Atlas V rocket. This was ULA's sixth mission supporting Amazon's satellite network.

title: Gatehouse Satcom and Rohde & Schwarz Partner on 5GN NTN Testing
summary: Gatehouse Satcom and Rohde & Schwarz have partnered to collaborate on 5G non-terrestrial network (NTN) testing, leveraging Gatehouse's software expertise and Rohde & Schwarz's testing capabilities.

title: HawkEye 360 Files to Go Public, Reports $99M in Revenue in 2025
summary: HawkEye 360, a signals intelligence company, announced plans to go public via an IPO in 2025, targeting $99 million in revenue. The company initiated a road show to attract investors for the public offering.

title: Pakistan Adds to EO Fleet of Satellites
summary: Pakistan launched the EO-3 satellite, expanding its Earth Observation (EO) fleet. The mission was conducted by the Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (SUPARCO).

title: Space Force Awards $3.2 Billion to 12 Companies for Space-Based Interceptor Work
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 12 companies contracts worth up to $3.2 billion to develop space-based interceptor technologies for the Golden Dome initiative. This move aims to enhance space defense capabilities.
